meta.title: My Hero - Full Song Flow
meta.subtitle: Connect intro, verse, and chorus into one performance.

objectives: Understand a simple section map for a rock song
Play through intro, verse, and chorus without stopping

steps.title: From riff to full song
steps.body: You've built a strong My Hero-style riff with power chords, muting, and dynamics. Now it's time to connect everything into one flowing performance: intro, verse, and chorus, just like a real song.

steps.title: Simple section map
steps.body: We'll use a simplified structure inspired by big rock songs: Intro -> Verse 1 -> Chorus 1 -> Verse 2 -> Chorus 2. No bridge yet - just a clean path that you can actually play from start to finish. Think of it as your own short My Hero arrangement.

steps.title: Intro groove
steps.body: For the intro, play your power-chord loop with open strums but a slightly softer touch than the chorus. You want it to feel like a build-up, not the loudest part of the song. Play 2-4 bars of this to set the mood.

steps.title: Verse pattern
steps.body: When the "verse" starts, switch to the muted version of your loop from the Grohl Rhythm lesson. Keep your 8th notes tight and controlled. Aim for 4 bars of muted verse playing before you think about "going big" again.

steps.title: Chorus pattern
steps.body: For the chorus, play the same chord loop fully open and at near-maximum strumming intensity. Remember to accent beats 2 and 4 so it feels locked to an imaginary snare drum. Start with 4 bars of big chorus playing.

steps.title: Practice the transitions
steps.body: Work on three key transitions: Intro -> Verse, Verse -> Chorus, and Chorus -> Verse. In each case, keep your right hand moving in time and only change your muting and intensity. If you pause or rush, slow down and try that transition again by itself.

steps.title: Mini performance run
steps.body: Now try this full flow: Intro (2 bars) -> Verse 1 (4 bars muted) -> Chorus 1 (4 bars open) -> Verse 2 (4 bars muted) -> Chorus 2 (4 bars open). Don't worry about lyrics - focus just on the guitar part and the dynamic story.

steps.title: Full My Hero-style flow
steps.body: Can you play the full flow without stopping, even if it's at a slower tempo? If your arm gets tired, that's normal - take a short break and try again. The goal is to feel comfortable playing for a full song-length chunk of time while controlling your energy.

steps.title: 🎸 You played the whole thing!
steps.body: Huge milestone! You just played through a complete, simplified My Hero-style song form with intro, verses, and choruses. This is what real performance feels like: holding the groove from start to finish.
